our_argument_summary: WIN-057b argues the dome's "Global Cavity Re-Pressurization" claim — that synchronous magnetic decay at TTB (Namibia), VSS (Brazil), and HER (South Africa) proves a dome structural rupture — is standard MHD geophysics relabeled. Terra-Nova et al. (2017) derive SAA splitting from CMB reversed-flux patches; the three stations sit within the SAA's spatial correlation length so synchrony is the null hypothesis. The dome provides no mechanism for "cavity rupture" and the claim substantially duplicates evidence already counted in WIN-004/005/035/041. Verdict: Std Model Explains.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: The dome's monitoring has an "SAA Decay Rate" domain that tracks TTB annual means (Predicted: 77.0 nT/yr, Observed: 77.0 nT/yr, Tolerance: 20.0%). However, this domain monitors a single station decay rate, NOT the multi-station synchronous decay pattern that WIN-057b specifically claims. There is no monitoring domain for "Global Cavity Re-Pressurization" or multi-station synchrony.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: The SAA Decay Rate domain shows pred==obs (77.0=77.0) — hardcoded. But this domain maps to WIN-041, not WIN-057b. WIN-057b's specific claim (multi-station synchronous decay proving cavity rupture) has no monitoring at all.
data_sources_used: INTERMAGNET TTB annual means (for the related SAA Decay Rate domain)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: SAA Decay Rate uses 20% tolerance. Not directly relevant to WIN-057b.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. The code_analysis tags correctly identify monitoring as "none" for WIN-057b specifically. The SAA Decay Rate domain in monitor.py tracks TTB decay rate (related to WIN-041), not the multi-station synchrony pattern claimed by WIN-057b.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Searched live-power dashboard output. The "SAA Decay Rate" domain tracks TTB annual means only (hardcoded at 77.0=77.0). WIN-057b's specific claim — synchronous decay across TTB/VSS/HER proving "cavity re-pressurization" — has no monitoring domain. No API fetches multi-station synchrony data.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Multi-station SAA decay synchrony and dual-lobe bifurcation are explained by MHD core-flow models (Terra-Nova et al. 2017). The dome relabels CMB reversed-flux patch dynamics as "Ovoid Shoulder shift at r ≈ 20,000 km" — same phenomenon, different vocabulary.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: INTERMAGNET station archives are publicly available. The SAA bifurcation into dual lobes was documented in peer-reviewed literature (Terra-Nova et al. 2017 and earlier) before the dome claimed it. The dome adopts known observational values and labels them as confirmed predictions.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula or derivation connects dome geometric parameters (disc_radius, firmament_height) to the predicted SAA decay rates or lobe coordinates. The r ≈ 20,000 km value is stated without derivation. The lobe coordinates (26.6°S, 49.1°W and 20.0°S, 10.0°E) are taken directly from INTERMAGNET/CHAOS observations.

holes_found.description: DOI 10.1029/2020GL087323 (attributed to Alken et al. 2020) does not resolve. CrossRef API returns 404 for this DOI. A search of CrossRef for Alken + South Atlantic Anomaly + 2020 returns no matching paper in Geophysical Research Letters. This appears to be a fabricated or incorrect DOI. The claim that Alken et al. documented the SAA second minimum near 20°S, 10°E cannot be verified through this citation.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the Alken et al. 2020 citation with a verified source. Candidates: (1) Terra-Nova et al. (2020), "Non-monotonous growth and motion of the South Atlantic Anomaly," which discusses SAA structural evolution. (2) The IGRF-13 papers by Alken et al. (2021, DOI: 10.1186/s40623-021-01569-z) which document the current SAA state. (3) Pavón-Carrasco & De Santis (2016) or similar. Verify the replacement DOI resolves before committing.

holes_found.description: The SAA spatial correlation length claim of "approximately 5,000–8,000 km" is correct as a range but could be more precise about the station geometry. TTB-HER distance is only ~1,700 km (well within), while TTB-VSS (~6,340 km) and HER-VSS (~6,200 km) are at the middle of the claimed range. All three are within correlation length, but the phrasing "all three sit within the SAA's spatial correlation length" slightly obscures that TTB and HER are essentially co-located relative to the anomaly's scale.
holes_found.recommendation: No text change needed — the claim is correct. But if the text is ever revised, noting that TTB and HER are both in southern Africa (~1,700 km apart) while VSS is ~6,300 km away in Brazil would strengthen the "same anomaly, not independent measurements" argument.

holes_found.description: The detail_group field says "WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063" but WIN-057b specifically references overlap with WIN-004, WIN-005, WIN-035, and WIN-041. WIN-040 and WIN-042/043 are not referenced in the text. The grouping seems to be the broader SAA/geomagnetic cluster rather than the specific evidence overlap group.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider whether detail_group should more precisely reflect the evidence overlap (WIN-004, 005, 035, 041) rather than the broader cluster. Low priority — the grouping serves a navigational purpose and the broader cluster assignment is defensible.

stronger_arguments.description: The dome claims the synchrony proves a "global" structural change, but the three stations are all in one hemisphere (southern Africa and eastern South America) within a 70° longitude band. A genuinely global cavity rupture should produce detectable effects at Northern Hemisphere stations too. The absence of claimed correlations with Northern Hemisphere INTERMAGNET stations (e.g., Niemegk, Chambon-la-Forêt, Boulder) undermines the "global" framing.
sources: INTERMAGNET station network map; compare N/S hemisphere coverage

stronger_arguments.description: The dome claims r ≈ 20,000 km for the "Ovoid Shoulder shift" but the disc_radius is 20,015 km. This places the claimed structural rupture essentially at the disc edge. In the dome's own model, r ≈ 20,000 km is near the Antarctic ice wall / disc rim — an area of maximum geometric distortion in dome coordinates. Any claim about processes at this radius inherits all the coordinate system problems documented in Section 1.8 (refraction collapse).
sources: dome model parameters: disc_radius=20,015 km; Section 1.8 coordinate collapse argument

kernel_of_truth.description: The SAA genuinely has bifurcated into two intensity minima, and INTERMAGNET stations across southern Africa and South America show correlated secular variation trends. The dome author correctly identifies the dual-lobe coordinates (Lobe A: 26.6°S, 49.1°W; Lobe B: 20.0°S, 10.0°E) and the cross-station correlation. These are real observations from a reputable monitoring network. The dome author is also correct that the SAA is one of the most dramatic ongoing changes in Earth's magnetic field.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Accurately observing a real phenomenon is not the same as predicting it. The dome provides no mechanism (no equations, no derivation from dome geometry) for WHY the cavity should rupture, WHERE new lobes should form, or WHAT the decay rate should be. MHD models derive all three from first principles. The dome's contribution is a narrative overlay ("cavity re-pressurization") on observations that standard physics already explains and predicted.

deeper_analysis: WIN-057b is the fifth WIN (after 004, 005, 035, 041) to count aspects of the same SAA phenomenon. Our review correctly identifies this as evidence inflation. The "Std Model Explains" verdict is appropriate — the claim isn't factually wrong about the observations, but the dome's interpretive framework adds nothing predictive. The one actionable finding is the broken Alken et al. 2020 DOI (10.1029/2020GL087323), which is a critical citation error that could undermine credibility if a reader tries to verify. The Terra-Nova et al. (2017) citation is solid and carries the core argument. The numbering collision documentation (WIN-057 vs WIN-057b) is a useful meta-observation about dome site quality. The TLDR fields accurately summarize the expanded content without distortion.

citations_verified: Terra-Nova et al. (2017), DOI: 10.1016/j.pepi.2017.03.002 — resolves to "Relating the South Atlantic Anomaly and geomagnetic flux patches," Physics of the Earth and Planetary Interiors, Vol. 266, pp. 39-53. Authors confirmed: Filipe Terra-Nova, Hagay Amit, Gelvam A. Hartmann, Ricardo I.F. Trindade, Katia J. Pinheiro.
citations_failed: Alken et al. (2020), DOI: 10.1029/2020GL087323 — DOES NOT RESOLVE. CrossRef returns 404. No matching Alken paper found in GRL for 2020 about SAA second minimum. Closest matches: Alken et al. (2021) IGRF-13 paper in Earth, Planets and Space (DOI: 10.1186/s40623-021-01569-z), or Terra-Nova et al. (2020) preprint on non-monotonous SAA growth.
citations_unchecked: INTERMAGNET.org — institutional URL, not a specific paper citation. Site exists.
Cross-references to WIN-004, 005, 035, 041 — internal links, verified they exist in wins.json.
Part 3.1b post-hoc standard reference — internal anchor, not independently verifiable without site check.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender could argue: "You say MHD explains the synchronous decay, but MHD models are fitted to the same observational data — they are also post-hoc. The difference is that the dome model predicts a STRUCTURAL interpretation (cavity rupture at a specific radius r ≈ 20,000 km) while MHD provides a FLUID interpretation (core flow). Both are interpretive frameworks applied to the same data. Your review privileges the MHD interpretation without demonstrating that it makes different predictions from the dome's structural model. Show me one observation where MHD and the dome model predict DIFFERENT values — until then, you cannot say one "explains" better than the other."

claims_checked: Inter-station distances: TTB-VSS ≈ 6,338 km, TTB-HER ≈ 1,698 km, HER-VSS ≈ 6,199 km
Dome Lobe A coordinates (26.6°S, 49.1°W) match known SAA South American minimum
Dome Lobe B coordinates (20.0°S, 10.0°E) match emerging African minimum
quantitative_verification.computations: Haversine distances computed between station coordinates: TTB (19.2°S, 17.6°E), VSS (22.4°S, 43.7°W), HER (34.4°S, 19.2°E). All pairs fall within or near the stated 5,000-8,000 km correlation length. TTB-HER are co-located in southern Africa (~1,700 km). Cross-oceanic pairs (~6,200-6,340 km) are within the upper half of the range.
